Mechanism described: Transmuting internalisation — thousands of cycles of empathic attunement; optimal frustration; and repair
Temporal implication: Thousands of cycles; cannot be compressed

Mechanism described: Regression to dependence and resumed maturation
Temporal implication: Developmental time; by definition not compressible

Mechanism described: Expanding capacity to stand in the spaces between dissociated self-states
Experiential learning of mentalizing capacity through attachment
Development of secure dependency on a relational timeline

Survives evidence critique?: Partially — observations robust; specific lateralisation claims contested

Mechanism described: Autonomic reorganisation from chronic defensive states toward ventral vagal flexibility
Survives evidence critique?: No — polyvagal theory described as untenable or highly implausible (Grossman critique via Tilson 2025)

Mechanism described: Bottom-up sequential brain reorganisation requiring patterned repetitive experiences
Temporal implication: Sequential; each level requires its own duration
Survives evidence critique?: Partially — triune brain debunked; Perry retains hierarchy as clinical framework

Mechanism described: Accumulation of moments of meeting in implicit relational knowing
Hierarchical processing with bite-sizing — sensorimotor memories closer to read-only memory
Temporal implication: Slower than cognitive change; small pieces at a time
Survives evidence critique?: Partially — hierarchy as clinical metaphor; Fisher's bite-sizing is clinical observation
